channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Trump Calls Off Witkoff and Kushner’s Travel to Pakistan for Peace Talks
item.description: Trump cancelled a trip by Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for peace talks. Trump Calls Off Witkoff and Kushner’s Travel to Pakistan for Peace Talks.

item.title: Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat
item.description: Viktor Orbán stepped down from the Hungarian parliament after a landslide defeat. The outgoing prime minister will not take up his seat after leading his party back into opposition. Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: Netanyahu orders IDF to attack 'forcefully' after
item.description: Trump cancelled a trip to Pakistan by US negotiators after Iran skipped talks. US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: 'Not worth 18-hr flight' - Trump calls off Witkoff, Kushner's Pak visit as Iran skips talks The Times of India.

item.title: Viktor Orbán steps back from Parliament after landslide loss, vows to rebuild Hungary’s ’national
item.description: Viktor Orbán will not take his seat in parliament and vowed to rebuild his party. Mr. Orbán said his party’s caucus in parliament would be “radically transformed” following the election loss, and that he would not take his seat.

item.title: Trump cancels U.S. envoys’ visit to Pakistan for talks; says 'does not mean restart of war'
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister met Pakistan's Army chief and resumed commercial flights from Tehran's airport. Iran resumes commercial flights from Tehran’s international airport for the first time since the conflict with the U. S.

item.title: Diplomacy Fails, Sanctions & Regional Conflicts Escalate
item.description: Iran and the US saw tensions rise as diplomacy failed and sanctions escalated. Iran-US Tensions Rise: Diplomacy Fails, Sanctions & Regional Conflicts Escalate english. punjabkesari. com.

item.title: Russia pounds Ukraine’s Dnipro for 20 hours straight, killing eight
item.description: Mexican authorities are investigating a car crash that killed two US agents, reportedly CIA employees, after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab. The agents were not permitted to operate in Mexico.

item.title: Trump cancels US envoys’ trip after Iran’s Araghchi leaves Pakistan
item.description: Trump cancelled a trip by US envoys to Pakistan after Iran's Araghchi left the country. Trump later suggests that next talks will be over phone, saying 'If they want to talk, all they have to do is call!'.

item.title: 4 Killed In Israeli Airstrikes On Lebanon Amid Fragile Ceasefire
item.description: Israeli airstrikes on Lebanon killed four people, targeting loaded rocket launchers belonging to Hezbollah. The strikes occurred amid a fragile ceasefire, with the Israeli military confirming the action. Israeli military said that it had struck loaded rocket launchers belonging to Hezbollah.

item.title: 'Iran Can Call Us Anytime': Trump After Cancelling Top Officials' Pak Trip
item.description: US President Trump cancelled a trip to Pakistan by top officials, citing confusion within the Iranian leadership. Trump criticised the Iranian government for "infighting" and said they could contact him anytime.

item.title: Trump cancels envoys' Pakistan trip, in blow to hopes for Iran war breakthrough
item.description: US President Trump cancelled a planned visit by his special envoys to Pakistan, citing confusion within the Iranian leadership. The trip cancellation is a blow to hopes for a breakthrough in the Iran war.

item.title: Iran FM Says 'Yet To See If The US Is Truly Serious About Diplomacy'
item.description: US officials cancelled a trip to Pakistan for Iran talks, hours after Iranian Foreign Minister departed Islamabad. The decision was made by former US President Donald Trump, who had planned to send a delegation to Pakistan for diplomatic discussions with Iran.

item.title: Reported US CIA agents killed in crash not authorised to operate: Mexico
item.description: Mexico is seeking details from the US after a car crash killed two US agents, reportedly CIA employees, who were not authorized to operate in Mexico. The incident has raised concerns about US activities on Mexican territory.

item.title: Joy and desire for change as Gaza’s Deir el-Balah holds elections
item.description: Residents of Gaza's Deir el-Balah voted in elections for the first time since 2006. The vote brought joy and a desire for change despite destruction, displacement, and genocide in the area.

item.title: Iran FM Abbas Araghchi and Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif meet in Pakistan
item.description: Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi met Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif in Islamabad to revive US talks. Iranian FM Araghchi met Pakistani PM Sharif in Islamabad as part of efforts to revive direct negotiations with the US.

item.title: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
item.description: Local elections were held in the occupied West Bank and one Gazan city, with Palestinians voting for new leaders. Hamas and other groups did not participate in the elections, limiting the vote's scope.

item.title: Trump cancels US negotiators’ trip to Pakistan for peace talks as Araghchi leaves Islamabad without
item.description: Trump cancelled US negotiators' trip to Pakistan as Iranian FM Araghchi left Islamabad. Trump cancels US negotiators’ trip to Pakistan for peace talks as Araghchi leaves Islamabad without.

item.title: Indigenous Speakers Are Booed During Commemorations of Australia’s War Dead
item.description: Indigenous speakers were booed during Anzac Day commemorations in three Australian cities. The disruptions appeared to target a widespread Aboriginal custom acknowledging the land's original inhabitants. The incidents drew attention to ongoing tensions between Indigenous and non-Indigenous Australians.

item.title: Explosions and gunfire as armed groups launch co-ordinated attacks across Mali
item.description: Armed groups launched co-ordinated attacks across Mali, sparking explosions and gunfire in the centre and north. The attacks are described as the largest jihadist assault in years, with witnesses reporting clashes.

item.title: Beijing Warns Europe As Chinese Entities Included In Russia Sanctions
item.description: China demands removal of its entities from Russia sanctions lists. China demanded an immediate removal of Chinese entities from the sanctions list. Beijing Warns Europe As Chinese Entities Included In Russia Sanctions.
